title: NOC 33100 – Dental Assistants and Dental Laboratory Assistants | GTR Immigration
description: Dental Assistants and Dental Laboratory Assistants under NOC 33100 play a vital role in supporting dental professionals across Canada. As oral healthcare services continue to expand nationwide, this TEER 3 occupation offers stable employment opportunities in clinical and laboratory settings. With growing demand in community clinics, private dental practices, and public health systems, NOC 33100 can provide a practical pathway toward permanent residence through Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER3 – Occupations usually require college training or on-the-job trainingMajor Group33 – Technical occupations in healthSub-Major Group331 – Assisting occupations in support of health servicesMinor Group3310 – Assisting occupations in support of health servicesNOC Code33100

duties: Prepare patients for examination and assist dentists during procedures
Sterilize and maintain dental instruments and equipment
Polish teeth and apply fluoride and sealants
Perform administrative duties (appointments, billing, supply ordering)
Perform intra-oral procedures where authorized (temporary crowns, sutures removal, vitality testing, etc.)
Cast metal alloys for bridges and dentures
Pack plastic materials to form dentures
employment_requirements: Completion of an 8-month to 1-year dental assisting programOR
Registration with a regulatory body is required in all provinces except Quebec and Ontario
Certification by the National Dental Assisting Examination Board is required for intra-oral duties (except in Quebec)
Up to two years of on-the-job training
International applicants must verify provincial certification requirements before practice.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $36,600 to $63,500, depending on province, certification level, and experience.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 33100 is stable, supported by:
Increased public awareness of oral hygiene
Professionals with intra-oral certification may experience improved employment prospects.
labour_market_demand: Dental Assistants and Dental Laboratory Assistants are employed in:

Although supply is projected to slightly exceed demand nationally, employment opportunities remain steady in provinces such as Alberta, British Columbia, Saskatchewan, Ontario, and Manitoba where dental services continue to grow.
Why Immigrate to Canada as a Dental Assistant?
TEER 3 eligibility under Express Entry (depending on program criteria)
Career progression toward advanced dental roles
Dental care remains an essential service in Canada, supporting long-term employment stability.
pathways: If you are qualified under NOC 33100, you may be eligible for immigration pathways to Canada through Express Entry or Provincial Nominee Programs.
Contact GTR Immigration today to begin your pathway toward permanent residence in Canada under NOC 33100.
